segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Welcome to Sprint 23 planning. Let's start by reviewing our velocity and setting a sprint goal.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Last sprint we completed 34 story points. Our average over the last 3 sprints is 31 points. Nick is on vacation next week so we should plan for reduced capacity.

segments.speaker: Nick
segments.text: Yeah, I'll be out Monday through Wednesday. I can take on about half my usual load.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Let's aim for 27 points this sprint then. What should our sprint goal be?

segments.speaker: Olivia
segments.text: I think the sprint goal should be launching the new onboarding flow. That's our highest priority item from the product roadmap.

segments.speaker: Paul
segments.text: Agreed. The onboarding redesign has been in the backlog for two sprints. Let's commit to it this time.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Alright, sprint goal: Launch the redesigned onboarding flow. Let's review the backlog items.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: First item: TICKET-401 — Implement new welcome screen. Olivia, this is yours. What's the estimate?

segments.speaker: Olivia
segments.text: I'd say 5 points. It's mostly frontend work — new React components, animations, and a guided tour widget.

segments.speaker: Quinn
segments.text: Does that include the API changes for the progress tracking?

segments.speaker: Olivia
segments.text: No, that's a separate ticket. This is just the UI.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: 5 points for the welcome screen. Next: TICKET-402 — Onboarding progress API. Paul?

segments.speaker: Paul
segments.text: 3 points. New REST endpoint, database migration for tracking progress, and unit tests.

segments.speaker: Nick
segments.text: Do we need a migration? Can we use the existing user_settings table?

segments.speaker: Paul
segments.text: I looked at that. The user_settings table doesn't have the right shape. We need a step-by-step progress tracker with timestamps. Cleaner to create a new table.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: 3 points, agreed. TICKET-403 — Email verification step in onboarding. Quinn?

segments.speaker: Quinn
segments.text: 5 points. This requires integrating with SendGrid, building the verification flow, handling edge cases like expired tokens and resend logic.

segments.speaker: Olivia
segments.text: Can we reuse the existing email verification from password reset?

segments.speaker: Quinn
segments.text: Partially. The token generation and validation can be shared, but the UI flow is different. I'll refactor the shared bits into a common module.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: 5 points. Running total is 13. TICKET-404 — Profile completion wizard. Nick?

segments.speaker: Nick
segments.text: I'd estimate 5 points normally, but since I'm out for half the sprint, can someone pair with me on it?

segments.speaker: Paul
segments.text: I can pick it up when you're out. Let's keep it at 5 points but co-own it.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Good. TICKET-405 — Onboarding analytics events. Olivia?

segments.speaker: Olivia
segments.text: 2 points. Just adding tracking events for each onboarding step. We already have the analytics library set up.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: 2 points. Total is 20 so far. We have room for 7 more points.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: TICKET-410 — Fix the payment webhook retry bug. This has been causing failed payment notifications. Paul, can you take this?

segments.speaker: Paul
segments.text: Yes. It's a 3 point fix. The webhook handler isn't idempotent — it creates duplicate records on retry.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Important bug. 3 points. TICKET-411 — Update dependencies and fix security vulnerabilities from the audit.

segments.speaker: Quinn
segments.text: I'll take that. 3 points — there are 4 CVEs to address and we need to test nothing breaks.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: That puts us at 26 points, one under our target. Should we pull in one more small item?

segments.speaker: Nick
segments.text: There's TICKET-415 — add loading skeletons to the dashboard. 1 point, good polish item.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Let's add it. 27 points total. Here's the sprint plan.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: TICKET-401, welcome screen, 5 points, Olivia. TICKET-402, progress API, 3 points, Paul. TICKET-403, email verification, 5 points, Quinn. TICKET-404, profile wizard, 5 points, Nick and Paul. TICKET-405, analytics, 2 points, Olivia.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: TICKET-410, webhook bug, 3 points, Paul. TICKET-411, dependency updates, 3 points, Quinn. TICKET-415, loading skeletons, 1 point, Nick.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Any concerns? Dependencies we should flag?

segments.speaker: Olivia
segments.text: TICKET-401 depends on TICKET-402. I need the progress API before I can wire up the guided tour.

segments.speaker: Paul
segments.text: I'll prioritize 402 first. Should have the API ready by Tuesday.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Good. Any risks?

segments.speaker: Quinn
segments.text: The SendGrid integration might take longer if their sandbox environment is flaky like last time.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Noted. Let's flag that early if it becomes an issue. Alright, sprint 23 is planned. Let's go execute!
